Class Trap

java.lang.Object
ca.sfu.cmpt276.group8.StillEntity
ca.sfu.cmpt276.group8.Trap
public class Trap extends StillEntity
Represents a dangerous trap entity on the game map that damages the player upon contact. The trap disarms after being triggered and goes through a cooldown period before becoming armed again.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class StillEntity

    position

    Constructs a Trap object at a specified position with a defined damage value.

    Draws the trap on the game screen, displaying either the armed (retracted) or disarmed (spiked) image based on its current state.

  • Constructor Details

    • Trap

      public Trap(Position position, int damage)
      Constructs a Trap object at a specified position with a defined damage value.
      Parameters:
      position - The initial position of the trap on the game map.
      damage - The amount of damage the trap inflicts. Must be greater than 0.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if damage is less than or equal to 0.

  • Method Details

    • getDamage

      public int getDamage()
      Gets the damage value inflicted by this trap.
      Returns:
      The damage amount (int).

    • isArmed

      public boolean isArmed()
      Checks if the trap is currently armed and capable of dealing damage.
      Returns:
      true if the trap is armed, false otherwise (boolean).

    • trigger

      public void trigger(Player player)
      Triggers the trap, disarming it and applying damage to the given player. This method does nothing if the trap is already disarmed.
      Parameters:
      player - The Player object that triggered the trap. Can be null.

    • update

      public void update()
      Updates the state of the trap. If the trap is disarmed and the cooldown period has passed, it becomes armed again.

    • draw

      public void draw(Graphics2D g2, int tileSize)
      Draws the trap on the game screen, displaying either the armed (retracted) or disarmed (spiked) image based on its current state.
      Parameters:
      g2 - The Graphics2D context used for drawing.
      tileSize - The size of a single tile on the game screen, used for scaling the trap visualization (int).
